Private Owner Briefing

Prepared for Mat Ishbia

Issue 02 / Game 01 Postgame / July 11, 2026

Suns Summer League Pulse

Final / Phoenix wins

Phoenix 81
-
Portland 79

Asset evidence arrived before system evidence. The individual production was real. The continuity verdict remains open.

The 5-Second Read / AVC Interpretation

The answers are uneven. That is useful.

Peat and Maluach supplied credible individual evidence. Phoenix's 24-point first half, 20 turnovers, and rebounding deficit did not establish the mature possession environment the pregame brief was testing.

Not confirmed

Did continuity show up?

Phoenix's response did. Its possession structure did not consistently. The team finished with 18 assists against 20 turnovers after scoring only 24 points before halftime.

Confirmed / Game 1

Could Peat enter the structure quickly?

Yes, for one game. Peat started and produced 17 points, six rebounds, and four assists without attempting a three. The role was meaningful without becoming the entire offense.

Unresolved

Did the frontcourt control the environment?

The production was substantial; control was not. Peat, Maluach, and Fleming combined for 42 points and 27 rebounds, while Portland still won the glass 49-44 and the paint 36-34.

Final / Phoenix

81-79
57 second-half points
10Q1
14Q2
31Q3
26Q4
Assists
18
Turnovers
20
Rebounds
44-49
Blocks
5-4
Khaman Maluach7-15 FG / 3-7 3PT / 7 turnovers
19 PTS / 11 REB
Koa Peat6-11 FG / 4 assists / 3 turnovers
17 PTS / 6 REB
Javonte Cooke7-12 FG / 5-8 3PT / zero turnovers
21 PTS
Rasheer Fleming3 offensive rebounds / 2 steals / 2 blocks
6 PTS / 10 REB

Carry forward

Phoenix changed the game after halftime. A 57-41 second half is legitimate evidence of in-game response.

Carry forward

Peat's four assists matter alongside the scoring. They are early evidence that his value can connect possessions rather than merely finish them.

Do not overread

The final score does not validate the full development environment. Phoenix was out-rebounded, lost the paint, and matched Portland with 20 turnovers.

Game 02 / Sunday, July 1212:00 PM Phoenix

Phoenix at New Orleans

Pavilion, Las Vegas / ESPN2. The useful follow-up is whether Phoenix can begin with the possession quality it found only after halftime against Portland.

Owner checkpoint

Does Game 2 reduce the burden on individual recovery by creating cleaner structure from the opening possession?

Finals through July 10 7 games
  • MIA 119 — MIL 86↗

    Miami posted Friday's highest total and widest margin.
  • IND 99 — CLE 93↗

    Indiana closed a six-point win.
  • BKN 91 — NYK 65↗

    Brooklyn held New York to Friday's lowest total.
  • HOU 97 — DEN 86↗

    Houston won by 11.
  • MEM 97 — CHI 96↗

    Memphis edged Chicago in Friday's closest final.
  • BOS 83 — TOR 80 OT↗

    Boston-Toronto was Friday's only overtime game.
  • LAL 96 — OKC 84↗

    Los Angeles beat Oklahoma City by 12.

Verified July 11, 2026. Statistics and schedules are NBA-sourced. Conclusions labeled AVC Interpretation are editorial judgments from public information. Uncertain claims were omitted.

Next Confirmation

Game 2 should test whether the second-half response can become the opening structure.

Accuracy over volume. If a fact cannot be verified, omit it.
